steps.title: Graph the shift directly
steps.content: Solve the original equation for $y$: $9x+10y=19$ becomes $y=\frac{19-9x}{10}$. In Desmos, graph that line and then graph the shifted line by adding $6$ to the whole expression.

steps.title: Read the new intercept
steps.content: A vertical shift up $6$ units keeps the same slope and adds $6$ to every $y$-value. The original $y$-intercept is $\frac{19}{10}$, so the shifted $y$-intercept is $\frac{19}{10}+6=\frac{19}{10}+\frac{60}{10}=\frac{79}{10}$.

steps.title: Match the ordered pair
steps.content: The resulting graph crosses the $y$-axis at $(0,\frac{79}{10})$. That matches choice A.
